title: Fried Rice
tagline: Takeaway-style wok fried rice — veg, egg, or high-protein chicken.
servingsNote: Base makes 3; chicken+egg version is the full high-protein build.
macroNotes: Macros shown are for the chicken + egg version (450 g thighs + 9 eggs, 3 servings) — about 50 g protein per serving, which is excellent for a rice meal.
Veg-only version: drop to ~330 kcal and ~9 g protein per serving. Egg-only (add 4 eggs to the veg base): ~400 kcal, ~18 g protein per serving.
To push protein even harder without more calories: use 5–6 eggs and cut the rice to 1 cup raw. Rice contributes only ~10 g protein total, so shifting the ratio toward meat/egg is the move.
Oil control: instead of adding more oil in the wok, splash in 1–2 tbsp water when things start sticking — restaurants do this in high-heat wok cooking.

items: 1.5 cups raw basmati rice (~3.5–4 cups cooked), cooked with slightly less water than usual so grains stay firm
Best case: cooled and refrigerated 1–2 hours or overnight
VEG: skip meat/egg, or add 100–150 g extra veg
EGG: 3–4 eggs, beaten with a pinch of salt
CHICKEN + EGG (high protein): 450 g boneless chicken thighs (juicier) or breast, cut small + 9 eggs beaten with a pinch each of salt, MSG and pepper
3 spring onions, whites and greens separated (or 1 small onion)
1/2 cup (70 g) beans, capsicum or baby corn, chopped small
1.25 tsp MSG (total) — this is the takeaway-umami trick
Few drops sesame oil, to finish

steps.title: Use cold rice
steps.body: Cook 1.5 cups basmati with slightly less water than normal so the grains stay firm, spread it on a tray to cool, and ideally fridge it 1–2 hours. Cold, dry rice fries instead of steaming — this is the single biggest factor for proper fried-rice texture. If your rice is fresh, at least spread it out and cool it fully first.

steps.title: Marinate the chicken (chicken version)
steps.body: Cut 450 g chicken thighs into small bite-size pieces and toss with 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1/2 tsp MSG, 1/4 tsp pepper and 1 tsp cornflour. Rest 10 minutes.

steps.title: Sear the chicken
steps.body: Get a wok or large pan ripping hot. Add 1 tbsp oil, lay the chicken in a single layer (don't overcrowd — crowding steams it) and cook 3–4 minutes until browned. Remove and set aside.

steps.title: Scramble the eggs
steps.body: Same pan, few drops of oil. For 9 eggs, cook in 2 batches: pour in, let them set slightly like an omelette, then break into large soft curds. Large curds give a better restaurant texture than tiny bits. Remove and set aside. (Egg-only version: use 3–4 eggs the same way.)

steps.title: Aromatics
steps.body: Add the remaining ~0.5 tbsp oil. Add 6 chopped garlic + 1 tbsp ginger and cook just 20–30 seconds — garlic first but briefly, don't burn it. Add the carrots and beans/capsicum/baby corn and stir-fry 1 minute (baby corn doesn't need pre-cooking; it stays slightly crunchy).

steps.title: Fry the rice
steps.body: Add the cold rice, break up any clumps and toss aggressively on high heat. For extra flavour, let it sit untouched for ~30 seconds to pick up a little char (wok hei) before stirring. Add 1.5 tbsp of the soy sauce, 1/2 tsp MSG, 1/2 tsp sugar and the pepper. Toss well for 2 minutes.

steps.title: Separate a plain veg portion
steps.body: If someone wants it plain veg, scoop their portion out now, before the meat and egg go back in. Season it with the remaining soy to taste and set aside.

steps.title: Combine
steps.body: Return the chicken, eggs and spring onion whites to the pan. Add the rest of the soy sauce and MSG (about 1.5 tbsp soy + 0.75 tsp MSG remaining) and stir-fry 1–2 minutes.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Kill the heat and toss through the spring onion greens, 1 tsp vinegar and a few drops of sesame oil. Serve immediately.

notes: Cold day-old rice, very high heat, and the MSG + soy combo are what make this taste like takeaway rather than home fried rice.
Chicken thigh stays juicier and is harder to overcook than breast; breast is leaner if you're cutting fat.
Baby corn goes in with the other veg — no need to pre-cook it. White vinegar added at the end gives a subtle tang; a little green chilli or chilli sauce warms it up.
If you accidentally cook the rice too wet, drain it, dry it uncovered on low heat 10–15 min, then cool/fridge it before frying.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
